posts:
How to find businesses that need a website in under an hour.

The exact workflow I ran this morning. 🧵

Step 1: Pick a neighborhood + niche.

Today: Houston restaurants, Greenspoint area.

Open Yelp → filter by category → sort by review count → open the top 20-30 results → check the 'Website' field.

You'll be shocked how many are blank.

Step 2: Score every lead.

HOT = no website + real reviews/photos = proven demand, zero digital presence.
WARM = outdated site or missing key pages.
COOL = solid site, missing a revenue feature (online ordering, catering form, email list).

Today: 11 HOT, 9 WARM, 5 COOL.

Step 3: Build your angle before you reach out.

Don't say 'you need a website.'

Say: 'La Lupita has 230+ Yelp photos and no website — your regulars literally can't share a link to you.'

That's a real, specific problem. That gets replies.

This whole workflow took 45 minutes.

The full system — prompts, scoring rubric, outreach templates, objection scripts — is documented and packaged.

$19 until Friday April 18. Goes to $29 after. Link in bio.

reddit.title: I found 11 Houston restaurants with zero website in 45 minutes using AI prompts — documenting the whole process publicly
reddit.body:
Been building an AI-powered lead gen system that targets local businesses that are 'digitally invisible' — good food, loyal customers, zero online presence.

Today I ran it on Houston restaurants in the Greenspoint area.

**Results from 25 businesses scanned:**
- 11 with literally no website
- 9 with outdated or feature-missing sites
- 5 with solid sites but missing a revenue layer (online ordering, catering forms, email capture)

**Most striking finds:**
- A taqueria with 230+ Yelp photos and no website. Their fanbase can't share a link.
- A seafood restaurant open until 2 AM every night with zero web presence. They're missing every late-night 'tacos near me' search.
- A food truck with 1 Yelp review and no phone number — just showing up and hoping people find them.

**The workflow:**
1. Prompt-based Yelp scan (targeted by neighborhood + category)
2. Manual site check on each result
3. Assign HOT/WARM/COOL score based on urgency
4. Generate a custom angle for outreach (not 'you need a website' — something specific to their situation)

Total time this morning: about 45 minutes.

I'm documenting this whole process publicly — wins and failures. Currently pre-revenue, running 3-6 outreach batches per day. Today is Day 1.

Happy to share specifics on the prompts or scoring rubric in the comments if useful.
reddit.top_comment: For those asking about the full packaged system — prompts, scoring rubric, outreach templates, objection handling — I put it all together here: [GUMROAD LINK]. Currently $19, going to $29 Friday April 18.

linkedin.post:
Most Houston restaurants I've looked at this week have the same problem: they're excellent at cooking and completely invisible online.

Today I scanned 25 restaurants in the Greenspoint area. 11 of them have no website at all. One has 230+ Yelp photos. One is open until 2 AM every night. Neither shows up on Google. Their customers can't share a link. They're losing business they'll never know about.

I built an AI-assisted system that finds these businesses in under an hour, scores them by how urgently they need help, and generates a specific pitch for each one — not 'you need a website' but 'here's the exact revenue you're leaving on the table.' The whole workflow runs on prompts I've refined over the past two weeks. This morning it surfaced 25 leads in 45 minutes.

If you're a Houston restaurant owner dealing with this — invisible on Google, stuck on an outdated site, watching foot traffic but zero online presence — DM me. I'll show you exactly what I'd build for your situation. No pitch deck. Just a real conversation.
